h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: I’d always thought that was just me being sloppy
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Sorry, I did mean “sloppy”, and that was the wrong frame because it makes it sound as if there is no rule at all. Better is: home rule first, essay form after, I think.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The prior turns were heading toward filling in the grammatical grid and testing acceptability contrasts as an empirical exercise, without reflecting on the framing language itself.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought `p < .05` meant the change was important enough to act on, but that is doing too much work.
scored.naiveTrajectory: After L1's correction of p-value overinterpretation and demand for arithmetic, the expected next move would be to perform or request that arithmetic on the new numbers.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I think my first instinct was still picturing one big flood doing the main work, because the canyon is so large.
scored.naiveTrajectory: L1 offers a tentative arithmetic answer and a lingering doubt; the expected next move is a clarification of the rate or the scale, not a self-diagnosis of a framing error.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought “presumed innocent” meant the court was basically starting from “he probably didn’t do it.”
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I think that is better, but “not guilty” still keeps sounding like “didn’t do it” in my head.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was heading toward accepting a corrected definition and testing it on examples, not toward re-analyzing their own past wording as a structural 'framing problem.'

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought fewer predators simply helped the deer because more of them survive, but with the paper deer crowded inside the same chalk boundary, that only explains the first round.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: My old framing hid the food limit, because I was only counting deer surviving and not asking whether the leaves and regrowth could support them.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue refining the content of their answer by adding carrying capacity and overbrowsing terms, staying within the forward problem-solving frame.

scored.recoheredEarlier: The clear water still makes me think the salt is gone, or at least not in there like before.
scored.naiveTrajectory: L1 leaned toward the idea that clear might mean not pure but remained unsure how the salt could be there without showing, pointing toward clarifying the unseen presence.
